title: Rajwada palace
tagline: Rajwada palace — heritage city of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Rajwada, also known as the Holkar Palace or Old Palace, is a historical palace in Indore that was constructed by the Holkars of the Maratha empire around 2 centuries ago.
overview.description: Rajwada, also known as the Holkar Palace or Old Palace, is a historical palace in Indore that was constructed by the Holkars of the Maratha empire around 2 centuries ago. An example of the architecture of the time, the palace is a 7-story structure that is placed near the Krishnapura Chhatri. The structure has gained the attention of tourists due to its size and age. The construction of the palace was started by Malhar Rao Holkar, the founder of Holkar Dynasty, in the year 1747 A.D. This structure is placed in Khajuri Bazaar, near the center of the city.

topPlaces.name: Indore
topPlaces.description: Indore ( ; ISO: Indaura, Hindi: [ɪn̪d̪ɔːr]) is the largest city of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh where it serves as the capital and the administrative headquarters of the eponymous district and division. Modern-day Indore was established on the banks of the Kanh and Saraswati rivers and traces its roots to its 16th-century founding as a trading hub between the Deccan Plateau and Delhi.

topPlaces.name: 2025 Indore drinking water contamination
topPlaces.description: 2025 Indore drinking water contamination was a public health crisis that occurred in Bhagirathpura, a locality in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India. At least 15 people died, and approximately 270 others were hospitalised after the municipal drinking water became contaminated, resulting in a widespread outbreak of waterborne disease.

topPlaces.name: Kanh River
topPlaces.description: The Kanh is a river flowing through Indore, the largest city in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. The river started carrying sewage in the early 1990s.

topPlaces.name: Malwa
topPlaces.description: Malwa (IPA: [maːlʋaː]) is a historical region of west-central India occupying a plateau of volcanic origin. Geologically, the Malwa Plateau generally refers to the volcanic upland north of the Vindhya Range.

topPlaces.name: Sarwate Bus Stand
topPlaces.description: The Sarwate Inter-state bus terminus popularly known as Sarwate Bus Stand or ISBT, located in Indore is the oldest and one of the biggest Inter State Bus Terminals in Indore. It operates bus services between Indore (Madhya Pradesh) and 5 other states, Rajasthan, Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra and Chhattisgarh.

topPlaces.name: Gangwal Bus Stand
topPlaces.description: The Gangwal Inter-state bus terminus popularly known as Gangwal Bus Stand or ISBT, located in Indore is one of the oldest and one of the biggest Inter State Bus Terminals in Indore. It operates bus services between Indore (Madhya Pradesh) and 2 other states, Rajasthan, Gujarat.

topPlaces.name: Devi Ahilya Vishwavidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Devi Ahilya Vishwavidyalaya (informally abbreviated DAVV), formerly University of Indore, is a state university located in Indore, India. It was named after Ahilya Bai Holkar, the 18th century Queen and ruler of Indore, daughter in law of Malhar Rao Holkar who was the founder of Holkar Dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Ravindra Natya Grah
topPlaces.description: Ravindra Natya Grah is a theatre auditorium and exhibition hall located in Indore, Madhya Pradesh. The theater is named after Rabindranath Tagore who became the first non-European to win the Nobel Prize.

items.note: Indore ( ; ISO: Indaura, Hindi: [ɪn̪d̪ɔːr]) is the largest city of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh where it serves as the capital and the administrative headquarters of the eponymous district and division. Modern-day Indore was established on the banks of the Kanh and Saraswati rivers and traces its roots to its 16th-century founding as a trading hub between the Deccan Plateau and Delhi.

2025 Indore drinking water contamination was a public health crisis that occurred in Bhagirathpura, a locality in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India. At least 15 people died, and approximately 270 others were hospitalised after the municipal drinking water became contaminated, resulting in a widespread outbreak of waterborne disease.
The Kanh is a river flowing through Indore, the largest city in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. The river started carrying sewage in the early 1990s.

Malwa (IPA: [maːlʋaː]) is a historical region of west-central India occupying a plateau of volcanic origin. Geologically, the Malwa Plateau generally refers to the volcanic upland north of the Vindhya Range.

The Sarwate Inter-state bus terminus popularly known as Sarwate Bus Stand or ISBT, located in Indore is the oldest and one of the biggest Inter State Bus Terminals in Indore. It operates bus services between Indore (Madhya Pradesh) and 5 other states, Rajasthan, Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra and Chhattisgarh.

The Gangwal Inter-state bus terminus popularly known as Gangwal Bus Stand or ISBT, located in Indore is one of the oldest and one of the biggest Inter State Bus Terminals in Indore. It operates bus services between Indore (Madhya Pradesh) and 2 other states, Rajasthan, Gujarat.

Devi Ahilya Vishwavidyalaya (informally abbreviated DAVV), formerly University of Indore, is a state university located in Indore, India. It was named after Ahilya Bai Holkar, the 18th century Queen and ruler of Indore, daughter in law of Malhar Rao Holkar who was the founder of Holkar Dynasty.

Ravindra Natya Grah is a theatre auditorium and exhibition hall located in Indore, Madhya Pradesh. The theater is named after Rabindranath Tagore who became the first non-European to win the Nobel Prize.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rajwada palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rajwada palace is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rajwada palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport, Indore (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhopal Junction (170 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rajwada palace?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rajwada palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rajwada palace?
faq.a: Stays in Rajwada palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rajwada palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Indore, 2025 Indore drinking water contamination, Kanh River, Malwa, Sarwate Bus Stand.
